stories.title: **Headline:** Ramírez Steals 300th Career Base — Now 9 HRs from the Exclusive 300/300 Club

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
José Ramírez stole his 300th career base Saturday in Cleveland's 14-6 win over Oakland.

He now needs just 9 more home runs to join the exclusive 300 HR/300 SB club. Only 8 players in MLB history have done it.

He might make it this season. #Guardians

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
The 300/300 club: Bonds, A-Rod, Willie Mays, Dawson, Beltrán, Bobby Bonds, Sanders, Finley.

José Ramírez is 9 home runs away from becoming the 9th member.

Not bad for one of the most underrated players in baseball. #Guardians

article.headline: José Ramírez Steals No. 300 — Now 9 HRs From Joining Baseball's Most Exclusive Club

stories.title: **Headline:** Nick Kurtz Walk Streak Ends at 20 — Tied Bonds, 2 Shy of Roy Cullenbine's 1947 MLB Record

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Nick Kurtz's walk streak ended at 20 consecutive games Saturday.

He tied Barry Bonds. He passed Ted Williams. He needed just TWO more to break Roy Cullenbine's 1947 all-time record.

The A's first baseman leads MLB with 33 walks. Historic. #Athletics

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Roy Cullenbine walked in 22 straight games in 1947. No one had matched it in 79 years.

Nick Kurtz got to 20 before his streak ended Saturday. He joined Bonds and Williams in the record books.

Baseball history gets made in the quietest ways. #Athletics

stories.title: **Headline:** Pirates Walk 7 Times in One Inning — Ties MLB Record from 1909 and 1983

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The Pittsburgh Pirates walked SEVEN times in one inning against the Reds on Saturday.

That ties the MLB record for most walks in a single inning by one team. The only other times it happened: 1909 and 1983.

Baseball is weird. And wonderful. #Pirates

stories.title: **Headline:** James Wood HR, García's 4 Hits Snap Braves' 6-Game Win Streak — Nationals Win 11-4

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
James Wood hit a 412-foot opposite-field HR and reached base 5 times as the Nationals snapped Atlanta's 6-game winning streak 11-4 on Sunday.

Wood has the MLB-leading 5th opposite-field homer of the year. The kid is for real. #Nationals #Braves

stories.title: **Headline:** Murakami at 13 HRs — On Track for One of the Greatest Rookie Seasons in MLB History

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Munetaka Murakami leads MLB with 13 home runs in his first 32 career games.

That's 3rd-most ever in the first 32 games of an MLB career.

His first 12 career extra-base hits were ALL home runs. A record since at least 1900.

65-HR pace. #WhiteSox

stories.title: **Headline:** Vargas Hits .388 After 27-Game Streak — The Legacy of Baseball's Most Surprising Story

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Ildemaro Vargas went 0-for-4 Saturday. The streak is over.

He still leads MLB with a .388 batting average.

The 27-game hit streak ended. The story of one of baseball's most surprising seasons in years is just beginning. #Dbacks

stories.title: **Headline:** Milestone Monday — Five Numbers That Could Define the 2026 MLB Season

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
5 MLB numbers worth tracking this week:

📊 Murakami — 13 HRs, on 65-HR pace
📊 Ramírez — 9 HRs from the 300/300 club
📊 Judge — approaching career HR No. 400
📊 Vargas — .388 BA, streak gone but bat isn't
📊 Rice — 1.214 OPS leads all of baseball
